title: $10,000 in Coca-Cola with DRIP (2004)
description: What $10,000 invested in Coca-Cola on January 2, 2004 would be worth today with all dividends reinvested. KO is one of the most famous Dividend Kings and Warren Buffett's largest holding. Berkshire Hathaway's Coca-Cola position, bought in 1988, now earns dividends equal to its original purchase price every single year.
dividend_note: Coca-Cola has raised its dividend every year for 62+ consecutive years. Annual dividend grew from $1.00 per share (2004) to $1.84 per share (2024).
buffett_context: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway bought ~$1.3 billion of KO in 1988-1989. By 2024, Berkshire receives ~$776 million per year in KO dividends alone — dividends that now exceed the original purchase price annually.
educational_note: DRIP accounted for approximately 32% of total return. Each quarterly dividend bought fractional new shares that then generated their own future dividends — classic compounding. Coca-Cola underperformed the S&P 500 over this period, but did so with significantly lower volatility.
